members.name: Bitbiashara
members.tagline: Empowering Tena estate entrepreneurs with Bitcoin education to unlock global opportunities for local businesses
members.description: Bitbiashara (Bitcoin + Biashara, Swahili for 'business') is a grassroots Bitcoin community project building a local circular economy in Tena estate, Nairobi. We actively onboard local merchants to accept Bitcoin, conduct regular education sessions, and host weekly community hangouts where people learn, earn, spend, and save in Bitcoin. Our approach focuses on simplifying Bitcoin through practical demonstrations so that anyone, regardless of technical background, can understand and use it confidently.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To pioneer the future of business in Tena estate by empowering entrepreneurs with the tools, knowledge, and opportunities to leverage Bitcoin in their operations, demonstrating how Bitcoin can enhance business operations, reduce transaction costs, and provide access to global opportunities.

Our Mission

To educate, empower, and support local entrepreneurs in embracing Bitcoin through practical education and user-friendly platforms, making Bitcoin accessible to everyone regardless of their technological expertise.
members.how_started: Bitbiashara began when founder Rosaline Wangui joined Bitcoin Dada and recognized the widespread misinformation about Bitcoin. As an entrepreneur herself, she set out to change the narrative by connecting with fellow entrepreneurs and demonstrating Bitcoin's practical value for business.

members.name: Afribit Kibera
members.tagline: Bringing financial inclusion to Kibera through Bitcoin education, merchant onboarding, and sustainable community programs
members.description: Afribit Kibera is building a Bitcoin-powered circular economy in Soweto West, Kibera – Africa's largest informal settlement where 80% of residents are unbanked. We create empowerment loops through our four pillars: Bitcoin education, sats-for-work waste management, women-led upcycling, and merchant adoption. We enable residents to earn, save, and spend Bitcoin locally, with 200+ residents educated, 50+ women earning weekly Bitcoin stipends, and 40+ local businesses accepting Bitcoin.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To build a self-sustaining Bitcoin circular economy in Kibera that brings financial inclusion, keeps value circulating within the community, and empowers local entrepreneurs – creating a replicable blueprint for informal settlements worldwide.

Our Mission

To empower underserved communities by providing the tools and knowledge needed to thrive in a decentralized economy. Through Bitcoin education, earning opportunities in waste management and upcycling, and merchant adoption, we make financial freedom accessible to Kibera residents through sound money.
members.how_started: In 2019, Ronnie Mdawida and Yogi, working under the Ronnie Fund, asked a radical question: Could Bitcoin offer Kibera more than just payments? Could it provide financial sovereignty? Inspired by Bitcoin Beach in El Salvador, they recognized that Bitcoin requires no bank account, no ID, and no permission – a tool for saving that cannot be inflated or censored. What began as a small experiment has blossomed into the Afribit Kibera Circular Economy, a thriving ecosystem where residents earn, save, and spend Bitcoin locally.

members.name: Bitcoin Githurai
members.tagline: Building a thriving Bitcoin circular economy in Toez Githurai through merchant education and community adoption
members.description: Bitcoin Githurai is building a grassroots Bitcoin circular economy in Toez Githurai, Kenya through a phased approach focused on merchant onboarding, community spending, and broader adoption. We onboard local businesses including cybercafes, electronics shops, barbershops, and restaurants, equipping them with Blink wallets, payment QR codes, and practical Bitcoin education through live transaction demonstrations and on-site community engagement.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To establish Toez Githurai as a thriving Bitcoin circular economy where local businesses and residents transact freely using Bitcoin, demonstrating practical financial sovereignty through grassroots adoption.

Our Mission

To build a self-sustaining Bitcoin economy through systematic merchant onboarding, community education, and practical demonstration of Bitcoin's value as a medium of exchange in everyday transactions.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Githurai began with inspiration and support from Bitcoin Beach, Bitcoin Ekasi, and guidance from Afribit Kibera. Recognizing the need for practical Bitcoin adoption in Toez Githurai, the team launched a phased approach starting with merchant onboarding. The first shops onboarded were cybercafes, where the team demonstrated Lightning Network transactions and Bitcoin's speed and low fees. From there, the initiative has grown organically, onboarding diverse businesses and building community awareness through education at each merchant location.

members.name: Bitcoin Dua
members.tagline: Empowering Agbozume's youth with Bitcoin literacy and financial independence to build a self-sustaining circular economy
members.description: Bitcoin Dua is a youth-focused Bitcoin education initiative in Agbozume, Ghana, centered around the Bitcoin Dua Education Center. We visit local schools to teach Bitcoin literacy, onboard merchants to accept Bitcoin payments, and host community events including football tournaments and workshops. Our goal is to build a self-sustaining circular economy where youth understand and utilize Bitcoin's value, supported by community farm operations that fund ongoing education and outreach activities.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To create a thriving ecosystem in Agbozume where youth are equipped with Bitcoin knowledge and tools, fostering a community empowered to achieve financial independence and shape its own destiny.

Our Mission

To educate and empower youth through Bitcoin literacy programs, school visits, merchant onboarding, and community workshops that demonstrate Bitcoin as a catalyst for financial independence and economic growth.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Dua began with school visits throughout Agbozume, impacting over 1,000 students and teachers within the first week. Recognizing the need for sustained education, the team established the Bitcoin Dua Education Center as a hub for learning and community engagement. The initiative has grown to include merchant onboarding, community events, and a sustainable farm whose proceeds support ongoing Bitcoin education and development.

members.name: Bitcoin Chama
members.tagline: Empowering rural Kenya through accessible Bitcoin projects like beekeeping and farming, building financial independence
members.description: Bitcoin Chama is a rural Bitcoin community in Kisii, Kenya, building a self-reliant circular economy through accessible economic projects. We empower young people and community members by introducing low-resource projects including beekeeping, poultry farming, and a community gym. Participants save their earnings in Bitcoin, protecting their purchasing power from inflation. We provide free clean water access, teach Bitcoin literacy, and onboard local businesses to accept Bitcoin payments, creating a self-sustaining ecosystem.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To build a self-reliant Bitcoin community in rural Kenya where people live daily on Bitcoin.

Our Mission

To empower our community economically through accessible projects like beekeeping, farming, and a community gym, while teaching Bitcoin literacy and encouraging savings in Bitcoin to protect against inflation.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Chama was born from witnessing a friend's struggle with inflation. In 2021, he joined a motorcycle chama (a traditional Kenyan savings group) and saved Ksh20,000 over a year to buy iron sheets for building a house. When he finally withdrew his savings, iron sheet prices had doubled – his money could no longer buy what he needed. This realization led to the creation of Bitcoin Chama, where community members save in Bitcoin rather than fiat, protecting their hard-earned money from inflation while building towards their goals.

members.name: Bitcoin Ekasi
members.tagline: Building a Bitcoin circular economy in Mossel Bay township through youth empowerment and merchant adoption
members.description: Bitcoin Ekasi is an extension of The Surfer Kids Non-Profit, founded in 2010 to empower youth in an impoverished Mossel Bay township where unemployment exceeds 50%. We pay our team of 11 surf coaches, teachers, and lifeguards entirely in Bitcoin while onboarding township vendors to accept Bitcoin payments, enabling coaches to spend their sats locally. We run daily Bitcoin education classes using the Mi Primer Bitcoin curriculum and operate a rewards program where children earn sats based on test results, fostering adoption through education and practical use.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To create a thriving circular Bitcoin economy in Mossel Bay township that empowers residents with financial inclusion and the tools to build a better future through Bitcoin.

Our Mission

To foster Bitcoin adoption through education and practical implementation, paying local staff in Bitcoin, onboarding merchants, and teaching community members to use Bitcoin as a tool for personal empowerment and financial sovereignty.
members.how_started: The Surfer Kids NPO has operated daily for over a decade, teaching 50+ children surfing and commitment to long-term goals. Inspired by Bitcoin Beach in El Salvador, Bitcoin Ekasi was launched to build a circular Bitcoin economy within this established community platform. By paying coaches in Bitcoin and onboarding vendors, we demonstrate that Bitcoin adoption grows organically when built on a foundation of education and trust within an existing community structure.

members.name: BitKwa
members.tagline: Building Ilorin's Bitcoin economy through education, merchant adoption, and peer-to-peer trade networks
members.description: BitKwa (Bitcoin Kwara) is a nonprofit Bitcoin-only community building a circular economy in Kwara State, Nigeria. Through our four core programs - Bitcoin Merchants Onboarding Project (BMOP), BitKwa Bitcoin Diploma education, The Bitcoin Hurdle meetups and conferences, and BitKwa Labs developer hub - we empower residents to earn, spend, and build with Bitcoin. We onboard merchants, train developers in open-source Bitcoin development, and create practical ecosystems where Bitcoin circulates within local communities, reducing reliance on inflationary fiat.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To build a financially sovereign society where individuals and communities in Africa live, work, and trade freely through sustainable Bitcoin circular economies.

Our Mission

To empower communities in Ilorin and beyond by driving real-world Bitcoin adoption through education, merchant integration, and circular trade networks, creating a self-sustaining economy powered by knowledge, innovation, and financial freedom.
members.how_started: After running the BitKwa Bitcoin Diploma and educating hundreds of people, it became clear that knowledge alone wasn't enough. Learners understood Bitcoin but had nowhere to earn or spend it. Merchants were curious but lacked guidance. BitKwa evolved beyond education to build practical infrastructure: merchant onboarding, peer-to-peer trade networks, and developer training. Kwara State's strategic location, large informal economy, and youth population made it the ideal launchpad for Nigeria's first living Bitcoin circular economy.

members.name: Bitcoin NextGen
members.tagline: Empowering kids and teens in Cross River State with Bitcoin education through school clubs and earn-to-learn programs
members.description: Bitcoin NextGen is a bold educational initiative aimed at empowering kids and teenagers in Akamkpa L.G.A with the knowledge, tools, and confidence to participate in the Bitcoin economy. Through creative learning methods including school-based Bitcoin Clubs, spoken word, interactive books, games, and real sats, we teach children how to earn, save, and spend using Bitcoin. Starting from age 8 and up, we make Bitcoin practical, not just theoretical, helping build a generation of young leaders who are financially literate, tech-savvy, and Bitcoin-ready.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To build a community where children understand money, freedom, and digital innovation through Bitcoin. We aim to create a generation of financially literate young leaders who are empowered, tech-savvy, and Bitcoin-ready.

Our Mission

To empower kids and teenagers in our local community with the knowledge, tools, and confidence to participate in the Bitcoin economy. Through creative learning, hands-on wallet use, and circular economy activities, we help build young leaders who understand financial sovereignty and peer-to-peer technology.
members.how_started: Bitcoin NextGen emerged from a recognition that kids and teenagers in Akamkpa LGA have limited access to formal financial education. The project aims to teach children about Bitcoin from a young age to prepare them for the evolving digital economy.

members.name: Bitcoin Ekiti
members.tagline: Spreading Bitcoin adoption across Ekiti State through education and helping merchants accept Bitcoin
members.description: Bitcoin Ekiti is building a Bitcoin circular economy across Ekiti State, Nigeria through education, meetups, and merchant adoption. We run the My First Bitcoin Diploma program, teaching students, merchants, freelancers, and artisans about Bitcoin fundamentals and practical usage. Through community meetups, we create spaces where people learn, connect, and grow with Bitcoin while onboarding local merchants to accept Bitcoin for everyday transactions.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To establish Ekiti State as a hub for Bitcoin education and adoption where residents understand and use Bitcoin in daily commerce.

Our Mission

To spread grassroots Bitcoin education throughout Ekiti State through diploma programs and community meetups, empowering merchants, students, and freelancers to adopt Bitcoin and build a thriving circular economy.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Ekiti emerged from a vision to bring grassroots Bitcoin education to Ekiti State. Led by Glory Ademilua, the initiative launched with community meetups and the My First Bitcoin Diploma program, bringing together merchants, students, freelancers, and artisans to learn Bitcoin fundamentals and practical applications. Bitcoin Ekiti creates educational spaces where adoption grows organically through knowledge and community connection.

members.name: Bitcoin Ikorodu
members.tagline: Empowering Ikorodu's youth and businesses through Bitcoin education, merchant onboarding, and community events
members.description: Bitcoin Ikorodu is building a self-sustaining Bitcoin circular economy in Ikorodu, Lagos where residents earn, spend, and save Bitcoin locally. We onboard merchants with Lightning training and signage, run work-for-sats programs where youth earn Bitcoin while providing value, and host community events including school programs and monthly meetups. Our goal is to create a closed-loop economy where sats circulate within the community, demonstrating practical Bitcoin adoption at the grassroots level.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To build a self-sustaining Bitcoin circular economy in Ikorodu where community members earn, save, and spend Bitcoin daily.

Our Mission

To empower local businesses, youth, and institutions in Ikorodu through merchant onboarding, work-for-sats programs, and school education initiatives that create practical Bitcoin adoption at the grassroots level.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Ikorodu was founded in 2021 by Destiny Smart, inspired by Bitcoin adoption stories from across Africa. Starting with a small team and a handful of local merchants, the initiative has grown to create daily Bitcoin transactions within the Ikorodu community through local educational sessions, merchant onboarding, and work-for-sats programs.

members.name: Bitcoin Dombo
members.tagline: Empowering Mozambique's youth to build sustainable businesses through Bitcoin education and entrepreneurship training
members.description: Bitcoin Dombo is a Bitcoin circular economy based in Khongolote, Mozambique, launched by Maputo Skate and Education Social Project. We onboard local merchants to adopt Bitcoin as a payment method and savings tool while empowering youth to start and grow small businesses that accept Bitcoin. Through workshops on Bitcoin wallets, payments, and digital security, we strengthen the local economy by connecting merchants and consumers through Bitcoin, encouraging spending within the community.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To adopt Bitcoin as a tool for financial inclusion and economic freedom in Khongolote, strengthening the local economy by connecting merchants and consumers through a Bitcoin circular economy.

Our Mission

To empower young people and local communities to start sustainable small businesses using Bitcoin as payment and savings, promoting financial and technological education through workshops on wallets, payments, and digital security.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Dombo was started by Maputo Skate and Education Social Project as a response to the need for financial inclusion and youth empowerment in Khongolote, Mozambique. Recognizing that young people needed practical pathways to entrepreneurship and local merchants needed alternatives to traditional payment systems, the initiative began onboarding merchants and training youth to build small businesses that accept Bitcoin.

members.name: BitJR Academy
members.tagline: Building Bitcoin circular economy in Zambia through education and local merchant onboarding
members.description: BitJR Academy & Space is a financial literacy program in Livingstone, Zambia educating young people aged 6 to 17 about Bitcoin and digital finance. Through engaging activities and a dedicated learning space, we teach children practical financial skills and create a kid-driven circular economy where young people can earn, save, and spend Bitcoin independently. We empower the next generation to take control of their financial futures from an early age, shifting mindsets to recognize children as future builders and economic participants.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To empower the next generation in Livingstone and beyond with practical Bitcoin and financial skills, creating a future where children become leaders, innovators, and economic participants.

Our Mission

To educate children and teenagers aged 6 to 17 about Bitcoin and digital finance through hands-on learning activities, building kid-driven circular economies that give young people the knowledge, confidence, and tools to manage their financial futures.
members.how_started: BitJR was created by Walusungu Nyachikanda, who grew up in Livingstone, Zambia witnessing how limited financial education was for young people. Recognizing that children were being left behind as the world moved into a digital economy, BitJR was launched to give young people practical knowledge and confidence with Bitcoin. Starting in Livingstone represents the belief that financial freedom should begin in childhood, and that the future of money belongs to every child who dares to learn and build.

members.name: Bitcoin Victoria Falls
members.tagline: Building a thriving Bitcoin ecosystem in Livingstone through education, meetups, and merchant onboarding programs
members.description: Bitcoin Victoria Falls is a Bitcoin circular economy initiative in Livingstone, southern Zambia, focused on education, merchant adoption, and community building. Through the My First Bitcoin course, regular meetups, and hands-on merchant onboarding, we empower locals with the knowledge and tools to understand and use Bitcoin. We work closely with business owners to integrate Bitcoin payments into their operations, creating a sustainable Bitcoin ecosystem in the tourism capital of Zambia.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To rekindle hope in southern Zambia by building a thriving Bitcoin circular economy where individuals and businesses can seamlessly transact, save, and build wealth using Bitcoin.

Our Mission

To empower the Livingstone community through Bitcoin education, merchant adoption, and practical implementation – creating a sustainable ecosystem where Bitcoin becomes part of everyday economic life.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Victoria Falls was founded by Adam Cowperthwait after his transformative visit to El Zonte, El Salvador in late 2022, where he witnessed the Bitcoin Beach project's impact firsthand. Adam saw potential in the Free Haven Community Initiative in Livingstone, which shared similarities with The Hope House in El Zonte. He connected with Free Haven's founder Samantha Knaub and President Forest Hall, and together with Jason Peck, they began laying groundwork in early 2023. With support from Ndesanjo Macho and Anita Posch from Bitcoin for Fairness, they built the right team. In 2024, Bitcoin Victoria Falls officially launched with Humphrey Simwinga as community leader. The project started with the My First Bitcoin course and monthly meetups at Jollyboys Backpackers. What began as an idea inspired by El Zonte has grown into a real movement bringing Bitcoin adoption to Livingstone.

members.name: Bitcoin Anambra
members.tagline: Leading Bitcoin adoption in Anambra State through community education and building a thriving circular economy
members.description: Bitcoin Anambra is building a Bitcoin circular economy in Anambra State, Nigeria – home to Onitsha Main Market, Africa's largest market, and Nnewi, a hub of entrepreneurial activity. We conduct educational campaigns, regular meetups, and workshops at tech hubs to raise Bitcoin awareness while directly engaging merchants to accept Bitcoin for everyday transactions. Through merchant onboarding, community education, and sats giveaways, we're positioning Anambra State as a leader in grassroots Bitcoin adoption across Nigeria.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To transform Anambra State into a thriving Bitcoin circular economy, leveraging the commercial power of Onitsha Main Market and Nnewi's entrepreneurial spirit to position the state as a model for Bitcoin adoption and financial innovation in Nigeria.

Our Mission

To educate and empower the local community through regular meetups, workshops, and outdoor campaigns while systematically onboarding merchants to accept Bitcoin – creating a sustainable ecosystem where Bitcoin is widely used for daily transactions.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Anambra was founded by Kester Ejikeme, a passionate Nigerian Bitcoiner focused on demonstrating Bitcoin's practical use cases beyond just investing. Recognizing Anambra State's unique commercial strengths – including Onitsha Main Market, Africa's largest market by size and volume, and Nnewi's concentration of wealthy entrepreneurs and the Innoson Vehicle Manufacturing hub – Kester saw the potential to build a robust Bitcoin circular economy. The initiative started with educational campaigns to help locals understand Bitcoin's benefits, then expanded to systematic merchant onboarding in Onitsha's bustling market. Through regular meetups, workshops, and direct merchant engagement, Bitcoin Anambra is working to make Anambra State a leader in Bitcoin adoption across Nigeria.

members.name: Bitcoin Sisonke
members.tagline: Building Johannesburg's Bitcoin circular economy through education and merchant adoption
members.description: Bitcoin Sisonke is a Bitcoin circular economy initiative based in West Rand, Johannesburg, focused on education and merchant adoption. We conduct Bitcoin educational programs and regular meetups while encouraging local merchants to accept Bitcoin as a payment method.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To build a sustainable Bitcoin circular economy in West Rand, Johannesburg where Bitcoin is widely adopted as a practical payment method and savings tool throughout the community.

Our Mission

To educate and empower our community through regular Bitcoin meetups and educational programs while systematically onboarding local merchants to accept Bitcoin.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Sisonke was founded by Retshepile as a Bitcoin education initiative in West Rand, Johannesburg. Starting with educational programs and meetups for people interested in learning about Bitcoin, the initiative grew as the team went deeper down the Bitcoin rabbit hole. Their knowledge and experience inspired them to evolve beyond education into building a sustainable circular economy, encouraging merchants to accept Bitcoin as payment.

members.name: Calabar Bitcoin Club
members.tagline: Building Bitcoin adoption in Calabar through education, merchant onboarding, and a legal fight for Bitcoin rights
members.description: The Calabar Bitcoin Club was founded to introduce a parallel financial ecosystem where residents could address daily financial challenges including inflation, financial exclusion, censorship, and remittance issues. We promote Bitcoin adoption through circular economy building, education, merchant onboarding, and skills development. Bitcoin presents a solution for building a brighter financial future for Calabar – not just as money, but as a tool for financial sovereignty and economic resilience.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To create a future for Calabar where individuals and businesses can confidently explore Bitcoin as a tool for financial freedom, financial sovereignty, and economic resilience. We hope to inspire the replication of successful Bitcoin circular economies in Nigeria and Africa.

Our Mission

The Calabar Bitcoin Club is dedicated to fostering widespread understanding and adoption of Bitcoin across Calabar by educating residents, supporting local merchants, and building a strong circular economy on Bitcoin.
members.how_started: The Calabar Bitcoin Club emerged from the passionate Bitcoin adoption work of founder James Otudor. Recognizing the daily financial challenges facing Calabar residents – including inflation, financial exclusion, censorship, and limited banking access – James saw Bitcoin as a solution for building a parallel financial ecosystem. By aggregating all his adoption efforts into one powerful force, the Calabar Bitcoin Club was born. The initiative launched Bitcoin education programs in secondary schools in 2023, organized community meetups including a Bitcoin Halving Festival that attracted over 10,000 attendees, and partnered with local businesses to accept Bitcoin. The club also advocates for Bitcoin rights through radio programs, newspaper articles, and notably, James's ongoing lawsuit against the Nigerian federal government challenging restrictions on Bitcoin access.

recent_posts.merchant: Chef Green Signature Local Cusine Hub
Chef Green Signature Local Cusine Hub
Chef Green Signature Local Cusine Hub

members.name: Bitcoin Aves
members.tagline: Building Bitcoin adoption in rural Akatsi, Ghana through merchant onboarding, youth education, and community empowerment
members.description: Bitcoin Aves is a grassroots initiative in rural Akatsi, Ghana building a Bitcoin circular economy through merchant onboarding and community education. We onboard local businesses – from barbers and tailors to food vendors, pharmacies, and electrical shops – showing them how to accept Lightning payments for everyday transactions. As professional teachers ourselves, we educate youth, students, and fellow educators about Bitcoin through community sessions, school meetups, and workshops conducted in both English and the local Ewe language.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To see Akatsi become a leading example of Bitcoin-powered living in rural Africa, where shops accept sats, farmers sell their produce for sats, youths earn in sats, and schools teach Bitcoin as part of everyday life. We dream of a Bitcoin Circular Economy, built from the ground up and owned by the people.

Our Mission

To empower individuals and communities across Akatsi and beyond through Bitcoin Education, practical adoption, and local leadership so they can take control of their financial future with dignity and confidence.
members.how_started: In late 2024, in the small farming community of Akatsi in Ghana's Volta Region, a quiet revolution began. A few teachers, students, and merchants asked a powerful question: What if we could teach our people how to save, earn, and spend using sound money? From that spark, Bitcoin Aves was born in early 2025. As professional teachers in the Ghana Education Service, the founders are committed to helping their community escape inflation and financial exclusion through Bitcoin. They started by onboarding local merchants and hosting community workshops, making Bitcoin accessible and understood in both English and the local Ewe language throughout rural Akatsi.

members.name: BTC Shule
members.tagline: Transforming Winteko village, Burundi through Bitcoin education, financial inclusion, and circular economy building
members.description: BTC Shule is building a Bitcoin circular economy in Winteko village, Burundi by integrating Bitcoin education, renewable solar energy, and community empowerment programs. We provide Bitcoin education to all households, reward community participation with sats, and support local entrepreneurs with Bitcoin-backed grants. Through vertical gardening for adults and Mi Primer Bitcoin classes for children, we create economic opportunities in a village with no traditional financial institutions or electricity access.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To build a self-sustaining Bitcoin-based economy in Winteko that can be replicated across Burundi and Africa, creating financially autonomous and environmentally sustainable communities that thrive through decentralized financial ecosystems.

Our Mission

To use Bitcoin and solar power to educate, empower, and uplift the Winteko community by providing access to essential services like electricity and financial inclusion that are otherwise out of reach.
members.how_started: BTC Shule was founded by Belyï Nobel Kubwayo, who discovered Bitcoin in 2020 and became captivated by its potential to empower communities. As founder of Free Tech Institute NGO, Belyï integrated Bitcoin education into the curriculum to equip Burundian youth with digital skills. His vision transformed after attending the Adopting Bitcoin Cape Town conference in 2024, where he visited Bitcoin Ekasi, Bitcoin Witstad, and Bitcoin Loxion in South Africa, witnessing firsthand how Bitcoin circular economies create economic resilience in regions with high inflation. Inspired by this, Belyï launched BTC Shule (meaning "school" in Kirundi and other African languages) in Winteko village – a community with no financial institutions or electricity access. By integrating Bitcoin education with solar energy, BTC Shule aims to create a thriving, decentralized community that is financially and environmentally sustainable.

members.name: Bitcoin Babies
members.tagline: Empowering Kenyan mothers with babies through Bitcoin stipends and a circular economy of 15+ local merchant partners
members.description: Bitcoin Babies is a foundation supporting mothers with babies under the age of one in Kimbo-Matangi, Kenya through weekly Bitcoin stipends and financial education. We provide mothers with an educational book covering infant nutrition and Bitcoin basics, empowering them to gain financial independence without relying on traditional banks. Through partnerships with 15+ local merchants who accept Bitcoin, we've created a circular economy where mothers can save, transact, and build financial independence directly within their community.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To create a world where mothers in underserved communities have the financial independence and knowledge to ensure healthy futures for their children, using Bitcoin as a tool for empowerment and building self-sustaining circular economies.

Our Mission

To empower mothers with babies through weekly Bitcoin stipends, comprehensive education on infant nutrition and Bitcoin, and a thriving circular economy of local merchants – giving women control over their finances and creating sustainable pathways to financial wellness.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Babies began in 2024 in the Kimbo-Matangi community of Ruiru, Kenya with a group of 13 mothers. The idea was to send mothers small weekly Bitcoin stipends (around $5) to help with their babies' daily needs. However, the founders noticed mothers were immediately converting Bitcoin into Kenyan Shillings rather than using it directly. This raised a key question: What if they could spend Bitcoin without converting it? To make this possible, the team began working with local businesses in Kimbo-Matangi, encouraging them to accept Bitcoin as payment. The goal was to create a circular economy where Bitcoin could move within the community. Today, over 15 local businesses accept Bitcoin for goods and services, and a second group of 10 mothers joined the program in 2025.

members.name: EcoBitz
members.tagline: Building Kenya's Bitcoin circular economy through merchant onboarding and grassroots community education
members.description: EcoBitz is building a Bitcoin circular economy in Nairobi, Kenya where Bitcoin meets environmental sustainability. We harness the power of Bitcoin to spark creativity through glass recycling, upcycling, and art projects that empower the local community. By integrating Bitcoin into our recycling and creative initiatives, we create economic opportunities while promoting environmental responsibility.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To build a thriving Bitcoin circular economy in Nairobi where environmental sustainability meets financial empowerment, demonstrating how Bitcoin can drive both economic growth and ecological responsibility.

Our Mission

To harness the power of Bitcoin to spark creativity and economic opportunity through glass recycling, upcycling, and art – building a circular community powered by sound money and environmental stewardship.
members.how_started: EcoBitz was founded by Kevin Makini in Nairobi, Kenya to build a Bitcoin circular economy where environmental sustainability meets financial empowerment. Recognizing the intersection of Bitcoin and ecosystem preservation, the initiative integrates glass recycling, upcycling, and creative art projects with Bitcoin, creating economic opportunities while promoting environmental responsibility in the local community.

members.name: Bitcoin Famba
members.tagline: Fostering Bitcoin adoption in Maputo, Mozambique through meetups, workshops, and Lightning education

members.name: College BTC
members.tagline: Empowering university students in Ghana to learn, earn, spend and build with Bitcoin through campus circular economies
members.description: College BTC is a youth-led Bitcoin movement bringing circular economies to university campuses. We empower students to learn, earn, spend, build, and spread Bitcoin in real life, from onboarding vendors and running meetups to teaching Bitcoin development and creating peer-to-peer opportunities. Our mission is simple: turn students into Bitcoin-powered builders, entrepreneurs, and leaders who shape the future of money and digital freedom
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To raise a generation of students who are financially independent, digitally skilled, and empowered to build with Bitcoin — creating campus-based circular economies that grow into global youth-driven movements.

Our Mission

To spark a youth-led Bitcoin revolution on campuses by teaching Bitcoin development, empowering students to earn and spend in Bitcoin, and building real peer-to-peer economies that give young people financial freedom and a voice in shaping the future.
members.milestones: Milestones Onboarded 3 campus merchants to accept Bitcoin payments. Hosted a major campus-wide meetup introducing students to Bitcoin. Educated and orange-pilled 100+ university students on Bitcoin and financial independence. Officially accepted into the Bitcoin Confederations network. Successfully hosted 3 student-led Bitcoin meetups across universities.

members.name: TofoBTC
members.tagline: Bringing Bitcoin to Tofo beach community in Mozambique through merchant adoption and grassroots education

members.name: Soweto BTC
members.tagline: Building Bitcoin adoption in Soweto's township through merchant onboarding and education

members.name: Bitcoin Loxion
members.tagline: Empowering Khayelitsha township through Bitcoin education, merchant adoption, and a garden project with Bitcoin stipends
members.description: Bitcoin Loxion is a project focused on building a Bitcoin circular economy in one of the largest townships in South Africa. They are driven by a mission to empower the community, and provide decentralized opportunities that will help to bring about long term financial freedom.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To transform one of South Africa's largest townships into a thriving hub of innovation and financial empowerment through a Bitcoin circular economy. They envision a community where every individual has access to decentralized opportunities, fostering resilience and collaboration.

Our Mission

To empower one of the largest South African townships by creating a Bitcoin circular economy. The project aims to transform lives through financial freedom and decentralized opportunities, by promoting a movement of community driven collaboration.
members.how_started: BitcoinLoxion began shortly after Adopting Bitcoin Cape Town. Initially, We held weekly meet-ups, but soon realized that many people lacked accurate information about Bitcoin. To address this, We partnered with Trezor Academic to offer classes in Khayelitsha. We also introduced the "My First Bitcoin" diploma program. In our efforts to establish a Bitcoin circular economy, we started a garden project where workers earn their stipends in Bitcoin, helping to circulate bitcoin within the community.
members.milestones: Our biggest milestone has been seeing people in Khayelitsha adopting Bitcoin. The real breakthrough came when local merchants started accepting Bitcoin payments for everyday needs. Additionally, harvest vegetables, selling our produce to the community, embracing Bitcoin as part of our business transactions. Emplimenting Bitcoin into daily life is a powerful step toward financial inclusion and economic empowerment in the area.

members.name: Bitcoin Karoo
members.tagline: Supporting Karoo's small-town businesses and residents with Bitcoin education and sound money principles
members.description: We are a circular economy based in the Karoo region in South Africa. A semi dessert area dominated by farming and agriculture. We are dispersed with small towns and owner managed businesses struggling under the pressure of a broken money system, rising costs, capital controls and regulation. Our project looks to educate merchants and residents in the principles of sound money and help guide and protect Karoo residents and businesses through the inevitable volatility and confusion of the transition to a global Bitcoin standard.
members.vision_mission: Basics principles we stand by: *We stand for self sovereignty *We stand for education first Our primary goals are: * Education on the global issue of fiat monetary debasement and the significant long term effects this has had on wealth distribution, cost of living, freedom and the growth of fiat based diseases. * Education on the core principles of the Bitcoin protocol and why this solves for these issues * Education on the safe accumulation and self custody of Bitcoin.

members.name: Bit-Fitness
members.tagline: Building Bitcoin awareness in Somerset West through fitness education and community health initiatives

members.name: Bitcoin Arusha
members.tagline: Building Bitcoin adoption in Arusha through cultural expression, combining education with music, dance, and events
members.description: Bitcoin Arusha: Revolutionizing Bitcoin Adoption Through Culture & Community Introduction Bitcoin Arusha is an innovative initiative designed to merge Bitcoin education with music, dance, and digital media, creating an immersive peer-to-peer (P2P) entertainment experience. By leveraging the power of art and community, we aim to cultivate a sustainable Bitcoin circular economy in Arusha. Our approach fosters real-world Bitcoin transactions while empowering artists, entrepreneurs, and professionals to integrate Bitcoin into their daily lives.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

&

Our Mission

Vision To establish Arusha as a thriving hub for Bitcoin adoption by integrating Bitcoin into cultural expression, commerce, and education. Mission To educate and empower individuals through accessible Bitcoin learning opportunities. To build a sustainable Bitcoin circular economy by encouraging real-world transactions. To connect professionals, artists, and businesses through strategic networking and events. To create an engaging cultural experience where Bitcoin fuels the exchange of creativity and value.
members.how_started: How It Began Inspired by Bitcoin Eksai and with the support and guidance from Bitcoin Kampala, Bitcoin Arusha was established in late 2024. Recognizing the need for practical Bitcoin use cases and awareness, we launched monthly educational classes and networking events to build a foundation for Bitcoin adoption in Arusha. By integrating Bitcoin with music, dance, and digital media, we are redefining how people interact with Bitcoin beyond investment—making it a functional tool for everyday transactions.
members.milestones: Milestones Bitcoin Education Launched – Monthly classes teaching 20-30 students the fundamentals of Bitcoin. Community Meetups Established – Regular gatherings of professionals and Bitcoin enthusiasts to foster knowledge-sharing and economic collaboration. Creative Content Development – Production of Bitcoin-themed music, dance, and visual storytelling to inspire and educate. Dance Battle Events Introduced – First pop-up battle event combining entertainment with real Bitcoin transactions. Annual Festival Announced – Planned flagship event bringing together top performers, Bitcoin merchants, and an expanded audience.

members.name: Bitcoin Kampala
members.tagline: Building Uganda's Bitcoin circular economy at Makerere University and supporting orphans through education and commerce
members.description: Bitcoin Kampala is building a grassroots Bitcoin circular economy in Uganda, centered around Makerere University—Uganda’s largest academic institution—and extending support to communities like the Orphans of Uganda in Bugiri. What began as a response to a tweet by Bitcoin Ekasi, calling for circular economies across Africa, has grown into a thriving initiative combining education, commerce, and social good. Since July 2023, Bitcoin Kampala has onboarded vendors, schools, students, and orphanages to use Bitcoin as a real medium of exchange. At Makerere University, students can now pay tuition and purchase supplies in Bitcoin, while merchants around campus participate in a growing Bitcoin economy. Programs like “Fruits and Eggs for Sats” introduce children to Bitcoin while ensuring proper nutrition. Beyond financial literacy, Bitcoin Kampala has secured land for farming, boosting food security and sustainability for orphans in Bugiri. Thanks to Bitcoin donations, the project has funded beds, desks, school materials, clean water systems, and renewable energy infrastructure. As adoption expands and partnerships grow, Bitcoin Kampala is proving that Bitcoin can be a tool for empowerment, financial sovereignty, and transformative impact across Uganda.
members.vision_mission: To create a sustainable and unrestricted bitcoin circular economy for locals.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Kampala Uganda was born out of the inspiration of Bitcoin Ekasi and Bitcoin Beach Brazil. A month prior to the BTC Prague 2023 conference, Bitcoin Ekasi issued a tweet challenging anyone who believed they could build a Bitcoin circular economy, drawing from the success of Bitcoin Beach in El Salvador. The offer was a conference ticket for BTC Prague, providing an opportunity for the right person to learn how to create a circular economy in their local community. Through a Twitter community poll, Brindon Mwiine B emerged as the winner, demonstrating his previous efforts in organising local Bitcoin meetups, educating the community about Bitcoin, and fostering adoption. He was awarded the conference ticket and embarked on a fundraising journey, utilising Geyser Fund, to cover his expenses for the trip to Prague. Against all odds, he succeeded in raising the necessary funds, getting a late visa and made it to the conference on the morning of the appointed day. Attending BTC Prague and immersing himself in the circular economies’ booth there proved invaluable. The knowledge and guidance he gained, particularly from the creators of Bitcoin Ekasi in South Africa and Bitcoin Beach Brazil, made the establishment of Bitcoin Kampala Uganda an inevitable and exciting endeavour.
members.milestones: Under the leadership of Brindon Mwiine, Bitcoin Kampala has significantly improved the lives of the orphaned children in Bugiri, providing essentials such as consistent schooling, daily nutritious meals, and the recurring “Fruits for Sats” program. At Makerere University, Bitcoin Kampala has established a thriving Bitcoin-friendly ecosystem, involving students, local vendors, and community stakeholders. The project has conducted over 8 Trezor Academy classes around Kampala and ensured mentorship and growth among active community members. Women's engagement in the local Bitcoin scene has greatly improved thanks to education initiatives by Bitcoin Dada, The Core, and now the School of Satoshi. Major achievements include: Educational Empowerment: Enabled orphaned children to regularly attend school, provided uniforms and materials, and created the foundation for the Starlight School project. Basic Needs Provision: Purchased 30 triple-layer beds, 90 mattresses, and 180 bedsheets for the orphanage. Community Initiatives: Onboarded vendors around Makerere University and launched Juicy B and Gorilla Sats to model Bitcoin adoption in businesses. Health and Nutrition: Improved diets through “Fruits for Sats” and “Sugar Free Juice” programs in Bugiri and Makerere. Self-Sustainability: Enabled crowdfunding campaigns for the orphanage and construction of a permanent home. Beyond Uganda: Helped launch Bitcoin Arusha in Tanzania, empowering a dancing community through Bitcoin.

members.name: Bitcoin Plett
members.tagline: Building Plettenberg Bay's Bitcoin circular economy with 100+ merchants and self-custody education principles
members.description: A forum to help guide and protect Plettenberg Bay residents and businesses through the inevitable volatility and confusion of the transition to a global Bitcoin standard. Basics: Take self custody. Bitcoin, not crypto! Seriously, Bitcoin only! Do not discuss price, predictions, or gambling/trading. Leave mining to the specialists. Evaluate ideas, not people. When in doubt, ask more questions. Feel free to advertise local businesses or events that promote and support Bitcoin education and awareness. Don't abuse the privilege. Stay humble, study Bitcoin, stack sats. Bring on the memes.

members.name: Bitcoin Ubuntu
members.tagline: Bitcoin-only education and onboarding in Swellendam, providing free resources and community events
members.description: Bitcoin Ubuntu is a volunteer organization in South Africa focused on Bitcoin-only education and onboarding. They offer free resources, consultation, and community events, emphasizing the philosophy of Ubuntu – "I am because we are" – highlighting the importance of community and shared growth.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

To empower individuals and communities through Bitcoin education, promoting financial literacy and inclusion. The group is inspired by the philosophy of Ubuntu, which highlights the value of a communal approach.

Our Mission

To provide free Bitcoin education and onboarding resources, through online education, consultations, meetups, merchant and charity onboarding, and running a Mi Primer Bitcoin light node. They seek to integrate the philosophy of Ubuntu into their educational efforts.
members.how_started: This volunteer organization was started in 2023 with a desire to provide free, high-quality, educational resources to the local community, so that they are better equipped to avoid fraudulent schemes and defend themselves against irresponsible government monetary and fiscal policies.
members.milestones: Bitcoin-Only Platform: Focused exclusively on Bitcoin, rather than other cryptocurrencies. Online Education: Provides free Bitcoin education through online resources. Consultation and Meetups: Offers guidance and community engagement through consultations and meetups. Merchant Onboarding: Helps local businesses adopt Bitcoin for transactions. Charity Onboarding: Works with charities to integrate Bitcoin into their operations. Mi Primer Bitcoin Light Node: Operates a light node and contributes to this open-source education network.

members.name: Bitcoin Witsand
members.tagline: Guiding Witsand's coastal village through the transition to a Bitcoin standard with education and self-custody
members.description: In the serene landscapes of the South Cape, Bitcoin Witsand stands as a transformative initiative empowering local communities and reshaping economies through Bitcoin adoption. Spearheaded by forward-thinking entrepreneurs, this project turns a scenic coastal village into a hub for decentralized financial systems, fostering inclusivity and financial independence.
members.vision_mission:
Our Vision

A thriving South Cape economy driven by financial empowerment, inclusivity, and technological innovation, showcasing Bitcoin as a sustainable medium of exchange.

Our Mission

To promote Bitcoin education and adoption within local communities, enabling businesses and individuals to leverage Bitcoin for everyday trade, savings, and economic resilience.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Witsand emerged as part of a broader movement inspired by Bitcoin Beach and Bitcoin Ekasi. These grassroots projects have laid the foundation for Bitcoin adoption in South Africa by demonstrating the practical use of Bitcoin in everyday transactions. Edwin Jones, a specialist programmer who relocated to Witsand, saw potential in transforming the area into a local Bitcoin economy. Beginning in 2022, through continuous education and community engagement, Jones successfully onboarded local businesses to accept Bitcoin as a preferred tender, proving its practicality in real-world scenarios.
members.milestones: 2021: Launch of Bitcoin Witsand, marking the start of a local Bitcoin economy in the South Cape. Onboarding Businesses: Many shops in Witsand now prefer Bitcoin payments, thanks to consistent education and outreach efforts. Partnerships and Growth: Collaborations with Bitcoin Ekasi and Bitcoin Ubuntu to expand the reach of Bitcoin education and adoption across the region.

members.name: Orphans of Uganda
members.tagline: Children's home caring for orphans in Bugiri, building a new home through Bitcoin crowdfunding and Fruits-for-Sats
members.description: Orphans of Uganda Children Center (OUCC) in Bugiri District is more than an orphanage; it is a family. Founded in 2009 and now guided by former resident Isma Byarugaba, the home nourishes, educates, and protects 77 children every day. In 2023 the Bitcoin Kampala community welcomed OUCC into the Bitcoin economy through the Fruits-for-Sats initiative, and a year later rallied again to save Starlight School. Early in 2025 Ray Youssef, CEO of the peer-to-peer platform NoOnes, donated 1 BTC, laying the foundation for a new home for these children. Now, with support from Bitcoin Kampala and Praia Bitcoin Brazil, we are running crowdfunding campaigns on Bitcoin Economy Confederation and Geyser.fund to bring this project to completion. Your sats are becoming bricks; help us lay the next one.
